title: Quotes
lede: Send a polished, professional quote in minutes — and let iMash do the chasing. Build it with line items, taxes, and discounts; text or email your customer a live link; watch when they open it; and turn one click of 'Accept' into a ready-to-pay invoice. This guide covers the entire quote lifecycle, A to Z.

steps.title: Open the Quotes list
steps.content:
Every quote you or your team creates lives on one screen — with live engagement signals so you always know who has seen what.

  1. In the top navigation, click CRM, then click the Quotes tab (or go directly to /pipeline/quotes). The counter pill on the tab shows your total quote count.
  2. The table shows one row per quote with these columns: Quote # (click to open it), Status, Total Amount, Quote Date, Valid Until (turns red with an (Expired) tag when past due), Jobs and Deal (jump links to linked records), Account / Contacts / Leads (who it's for), Title, Sent, Views, Accepted, and Owner.
  3. Each row ends with four action icons: View (eye) opens the quote, Send (paper plane) opens the send composer for drafts, Download PDF downloads it with your default template, and Delete (trash) removes it after a confirmation.
  4. Tick the checkboxes on the left to select several quotes, then use the red Delete N selected button that appears in the header to remove them in bulk.

A quote moves through six statuses: Draft (not sent yet), Sent, Viewed (the customer opened the link), Accepted, Declined, and Expired.

💡 The Views column is your buying-intent radar: it counts every time the customer opens the public quote link, and hovering it shows the first and last view times. A quote viewed five times is begging for a follow-up call.

images.alt: Quotes list showing quote numbers, statuses, totals, dates and per-row actions
images.caption: The Quotes list — statuses, totals, engagement columns, and per-row View / Send / PDF / Delete actions

images.alt: Quotes list showing quote numbers, statuses, totals, dates and per-row actions
images.caption: The Quotes list — statuses, totals, engagement columns, and per-row View / Send / PDF / Delete actions

steps.title: Search, filter, and sort your quotes
steps.content:
1. Click Show Filters in the top-right of the list. A filter row appears with four controls:
  • Search quotes... — free-text search across your quotes.
  • Filter by status — show only Draft, Sent, Viewed, Accepted, Declined, or Expired quotes.
  • Start date → End date — limit the list to a quote-date range.
  • Filter by owner — see only quotes created by a specific team member.

You can also filter and sort straight from the table: click the funnel icon on the Status column header to multi-select statuses, and click the Total Amount, Quote Date, or Views headers to sort. All filtering and sorting runs against your full quote set, not just the visible page.

images.alt: Quotes list with the filter row open showing search, status, date range and owner filters
images.caption: Click Show Filters to reveal search, status, date-range, and owner filters

images.alt: Quotes list with the filter row open showing search, status, date range and owner filters
images.caption: Click Show Filters to reveal search, status, date-range, and owner filters

steps.title: Start a new quote — Customer Info
steps.content:
The quote builder is a four-step wizard: Customer Info → Line Items → Terms → Review.

  1. Click Create Quote in the top-right of the list.
  2. Enter a Quote Title (required) — this is what the customer sees as the headline, e.g. "Kitchen Remodel — Cabinet Package".
  3. Optionally add a Description summarizing the work.
  4. Link the quote to your pipeline (all optional):
    • Associated Deal — attaches the quote to an existing deal and automatically pulls in that deal's account, contacts, and leads.
    • Associated Job — attaches it to a field-service job; if the job has a quoted amount, iMash automatically adds it as a line item in the next step.
    • Account, Contacts, Leads — pick who the quote is for. Click the + button next to any picker to create a brand-new account, contact, or lead without leaving the wizard.
  5. Set Valid Until — the expiry date. After this date the quote shows as Expired and can no longer be accepted online.
  6. Pick the Currency (USD, EUR, GBP, or CAD).
  7. Click Next.
⚠️ A quote must be addressed to at least one account, contact, or lead before it can be created — if you skip this, the final Create Quote button will ask you to add one.

images.alt: Create Quote wizard step one with title, description, deal, job, account, contacts, leads, valid until and currency fields
images.caption: Step 1 — Customer Info: title, linked records, who it's for, expiry date, and currency

images.alt: Create Quote wizard step one with title, description, deal, job, account, contacts, leads, valid until and currency fields
images.caption: Step 1 — Customer Info: title, linked records, who it's for, expiry date, and currency

steps.title: Add line items
steps.content:
Line items are the heart of the quote — what you're selling and for how much. You have two ways to add them:

  1. Click Add Line Item for a custom item. In the modal, fill in:
    • Item Name / Title (required) — e.g. "Shaker cabinet set (12 units)".
    • Description — optional detail line shown under the name.
    • Quantity (required).
    • Unit Price (required) — tip: a negative price creates a credit line that reduces the total.
    • Discount (%) — per-line discount.
    • Tax (%) — per-line tax rate, e.g. 8.25 for your local sales tax.
    The panel at the bottom live-calculates Subtotal, After Discount, and Total (with tax) as you type. Click OK to add the line.
  2. Or click Select from Catalog to pull items straight from your Products and Services catalogs — names, descriptions, and prices fill in automatically.
💡 Build your catalogs once (CRM → Products / Services) and quoting becomes a 30-second job — no retyping prices, no typos in front of customers.

images.alt: Add Line Item modal with name, description, quantity, unit price, discount and tax fields plus a live total
images.caption: The Add Line Item modal — the footer live-calculates subtotal, discount, and tax as you type

images.alt: Add Line Item modal with name, description, quantity, unit price, discount and tax fields plus a live total
images.caption: The Add Line Item modal — the footer live-calculates subtotal, discount, and tax as you type

steps.title: Fine-tune pricing, discounts, and taxes
steps.content:
With items on the table, everything stays editable inline:

  • Edit any line's Unit Price, Discount %, or Tax % directly in its table cell — totals recalculate instantly.
  • Use the arrow buttons in the Actions column to reorder lines, the pencil to reopen the edit modal, and the trash icon to remove a line.
  • In the totals block below the table, type into the Discount ( %) or Tax ( %) boxes to apply one percentage to every line at once — perfect for a flat sales-tax rate or a negotiated overall discount. If lines have different rates, the box shows mixed.
  • The summary rows show Subtotal, Discount, Tax, and the Grand Total your customer will see.

When the numbers look right, click Next.

images.alt: Line Items step showing two items, inline price, discount and tax editors, and the totals block with grand total
images.caption: Step 2 — Line Items: inline editors per line, bulk Discount/Tax % boxes, and the Grand Total

images.alt: Line Items step showing two items, inline price, discount and tax editors, and the totals block with grand total
images.caption: Step 2 — Line Items: inline editors per line, bulk Discount/Tax % boxes, and the Grand Total

steps.title: Set terms, conditions, and notes
steps.content:
Step 3 is the fine print — everything here prints on the quote PDF except the last field:

  1. Payment Terms — e.g. "50% deposit due on acceptance; balance due on completion."
  2. Terms & Conditions — warranty language, validity window, cancellation policy.
  3. Footer Text — a closing line printed at the bottom of the PDF (e.g. "Thank you for your business!").
  4. Internal Notes — visible only to your team, never to the customer.
  5. Click Next.
💡 Set default payment terms, conditions, and footer text once under CRM → Settings → Invoices & Quotes — the wizard pre-fills them on every new quote, and you can still edit per quote.

images.alt: Terms step with payment terms, terms and conditions, footer text and internal notes fields
images.caption: Step 3 — Terms: payment terms, conditions, footer text, and team-only internal notes

images.alt: Terms step with payment terms, terms and conditions, footer text and internal notes fields
images.caption: Step 3 — Terms: payment terms, conditions, footer text, and team-only internal notes

steps.title: Review and create the quote
steps.content:
1. The Review step shows three summary cards — Quote Summary (title, links, expiry, currency), Line Items Summary (item count, subtotal, discount, tax, total), and Terms & Notes. Use Previous to go back and fix anything.
  2. Click Create Quote. The quote is saved as a Draft with an automatically generated quote number (the prefix comes from your numbering settings in CRM → Settings).

iMash keeps your pipeline in sync automatically: creating a quote for a contact links it into your deal pipeline, and quote/deal/invoice records stay connected so nothing falls through the cracks.

images.alt: Review step showing the quote summary, line items summary and terms cards before creating
images.caption: Step 4 — Review: one last look at everything before the quote is created as a draft

images.alt: Review step showing the quote summary, line items summary and terms cards before creating
images.caption: Step 4 — Review: one last look at everything before the quote is created as a draft

steps.title: Inside a quote — the detail view
steps.content:
Click any quote number in the list to open its detail view. Across the top you'll find the status badge and the action bar: Approve & Invoice, the send button (Quote for drafts, Resend Quote after sending), Preview, PDF, Edit, and a More menu (Share, Mark Accepted, Mark Declined, and View Invoice once one exists).

  1. The Overview tab opens with four stat tiles — Total Amount, Subtotal, Tax, Discount — followed by every detail: title, description, status, currency, linked Deal/Job/Account/Contacts/Leads, dates, and the engagement trail (Sent At, Viewed At, View Count, Accepted Date).
  2. The Line Items tab shows the full item table with subtotal, discount, tax, and grand total.
  3. The Terms & Conditions tab holds the payment terms, conditions, footer text, and internal notes.
  4. Click Edit to change anything inline — items, terms, links, dates — then Save Changes.
⚠️ Once a quote is Accepted (or converted to an invoice), its line items and totals are locked — the customer approved those exact amounts. The button becomes Edit Overview, letting you still update the title, dates, and linked records without touching the money.

images.alt: Quote detail overview tab with stat tiles for total, subtotal, tax and discount plus the full details table
images.caption: The quote detail — stat tiles, full details, and the engagement trail (sent / viewed / view count)

images.alt: Quote detail overview tab with stat tiles for total, subtotal, tax and discount plus the full details table
images.caption: The quote detail — stat tiles, full details, and the engagement trail (sent / viewed / view count)

images.alt: Quote detail line items tab showing the item table with subtotal, tax and grand total
images.caption: The Line Items tab — the exact table your customer's PDF is built from

images.alt: Quote detail line items tab showing the item table with subtotal, tax and grand total
images.caption: The Line Items tab — the exact table your customer's PDF is built from

steps.title: Pick a PDF template that matches your brand
steps.content:
Your quote renders as a professional PDF, and you choose the design:

  1. On the Quotes list, click Preview Templates. The Live PDF Template Preview opens with your real quote data rendered in the selected design.
  2. Use the Template dropdown to flip through the built-in designs (Classic, Modern, and more) — the preview re-renders live. Zoom In / Zoom Out to inspect details.
  3. Click Use This Template to save it as your default for all quotes — downloads, emails, and the public quote page will all use it.
  4. Click Customize to tweak branding, or Download PDF to grab a copy immediately.

Need a different look for one specific quote? Open that quote and click Preview in its action bar — selecting a template there saves it for that quote only. The company name, logo, address, and contact details on the PDF come from CRM → Settings → Company.

images.alt: Live PDF Template Preview modal showing the quote rendered in the Classic template with template picker and zoom controls
images.caption: Preview Templates — see your actual quote in every design before you commit

images.alt: Live PDF Template Preview modal showing the quote rendered in the Classic template with template picker and zoom controls
images.caption: Preview Templates — see your actual quote in every design before you commit

steps.title: Send it — by email, text message, or both
steps.content:
This is where iMash pulls ahead: one composer sends your quote by email, by SMS ("Text Quote"), or both at once.

  1. Open the quote and click the blue send button (Quote — it becomes Resend Quote after the first send). You can also click the paper-plane icon on a draft row in the list.
  2. Pick the channel with the toggle at the top: Email, Text (SMS), or Email + SMS.
  3. For email, review:
    • From — the sending mailbox (set up under Messages → Email).
    • Delivery Method — Send link to view quote online (recommended) lets the customer view and accept in their browser and powers view tracking; Attach PDF to email sends a static file instead.
    • To — pre-filled with the quote's contacts and leads; add more, or click Show CC/BCC.
    • Subject and Email Body — pre-written for you; click Generate with AI or Rewrite to let AI polish the message.
  4. For SMS, the panel shows Send to with the customer's numbers pre-checked (add extras via Add a custom phone number) and a pre-filled Message containing the quote total and the view link.
  5. Click Send Email, Send SMS, or Send Email & SMS. The quote's status flips to Sent.
⚠️ Texting a quote requires a default SMS number — if the panel warns No SMS sender configured, set one under Settings → Messages (or on the job source) first.

images.alt: Send Quote composer showing the Email / Text SMS / Email + SMS channel toggle, delivery method, recipients, subject and AI-assisted body
images.caption: The Send Quote composer — channel toggle up top, link-or-PDF delivery, and AI writing help

images.alt: Send Quote composer showing the Email / Text SMS / Email + SMS channel toggle, delivery method, recipients, subject and AI-assisted body
images.caption: The Send Quote composer — channel toggle up top, link-or-PDF delivery, and AI writing help

images.alt: Text SMS channel of the Send Quote composer with recipient checkboxes and a pre-filled message containing the quote link
images.caption: Text Quote — recipients pre-checked, message pre-written with the total and the live quote link

images.alt: Text SMS channel of the Send Quote composer with recipient checkboxes and a pre-filled message containing the quote link
images.caption: Text Quote — recipients pre-checked, message pre-written with the total and the live quote link

steps.title: Share the public quote link anywhere
steps.content:
Every quote has a public web link you can drop into any channel — WhatsApp, a chat, a QR code on a printout.

  1. Open the quote, click More, then Share.
  2. Copy the Public quote link with the Copy button. Customers don't need an iMash account — the link just works.
  3. Use the Public view toggle to disable the link at any time (for example after a renegotiation). Anyone visiting a disabled link sees a "this quote isn't available" notice; flip the toggle back on to re-enable it.

Every visit to the link stamps Viewed At, bumps the View Count, and moves a Sent quote to Viewed — so your pipeline reflects real customer attention.

images.alt: Share Quote modal with the public view toggle and the copyable public quote link
images.caption: Share — copy the public link, or kill it instantly with the Public view toggle

images.alt: Share Quote modal with the public view toggle and the copyable public quote link
images.caption: Share — copy the public link, or kill it instantly with the Public view toggle

steps.title: What your customer sees
steps.content:
The public quote page is your closer — polished, branded, and one tap away from a yes:

  • The full quote renders in your chosen PDF template with your company branding, right in the browser — with zoom controls and a Download PDF button.
  • A sticky bar at the bottom asks "Ready to respond?" with two buttons: Accept Quote and Decline.
  • When the customer clicks Accept Quote, the quote flips to Accepted, the accepted date is stamped, and an invoice is created automatically with every line item copied over — the customer is offered a link to pay it right away.
  • If they click Decline, the quote is marked Declined so you know to follow up with a revised offer.
  • Past the Valid Until date the page shows the quote as Expired and the respond buttons disappear.
💡 From a text message on their phone to an accepted quote and a payable invoice — the whole loop can happen in under a minute, without a single call back to your office.

images.alt: Public quote page showing the rendered PDF and the sticky Accept Quote and Decline buttons
images.caption: The public quote page — branded PDF in the browser with one-tap Accept / Decline

images.alt: Public quote page showing the rendered PDF and the sticky Accept Quote and Decline buttons
images.caption: The public quote page — branded PDF in the browser with one-tap Accept / Decline

steps.title: Approve and convert to an invoice
steps.content:
An accepted quote becomes an invoice — either automatically or by hand:

  1. Customer accepts online — nothing to do: the invoice is created for you the moment they click Accept Quote.
  2. You accept on their behalf (a verbal yes on a call): open the quote and click the green Approve & Invoice button. A confirmation explains it will mark the quote accepted and create a linked invoice with all line items copied over — click Approve & Create Invoice and you land directly on the new invoice.
  3. Just flag the status: use More → Mark Accepted (or Mark Declined) on a sent quote without creating an invoice yet; a Convert to Invoice button then appears for when you're ready.

Once converted, the quote and invoice stay linked — More → View Invoice jumps straight to it, and the quote's money fields lock to match what the customer approved. You've just closed the loop: quoted, accepted, invoiced — time to get paid.

images.alt: Approve Quote and Create Invoice confirmation dialog
images.caption: Approve & Invoice — one click marks the quote accepted and spins up the linked invoice

images.alt: Approve Quote and Create Invoice confirmation dialog
images.caption: Approve & Invoice — one click marks the quote accepted and spins up the linked invoice

support.title: Need Help?
support.content: Questions about quotes, templates, or the public accept flow? Email us at support@imash.io — we're happy to help you get your first quote out the door.
